content.full_text:
Legal Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for Informal Advice

Greetings:

Antioch Mayor Joel Keller has authorized and
requested me to seek the FPPC's opinion on the following
matter. A copy of your response can be mailed to Mayor Keller
at P. O. Box 130, Antioch, California 94509-0504, although I
would like either the original or a copy as well.

Mayor Keller is a member of the Kaiser Permanente
system for health care. Two other members of the five-member
City Council are also members of Kaiser Permanente.

The City of Antioch offers Kaiser as one of three
health insurance plans for both employees and elected
officials like Mayor Keller. Mayor Keller and the other
members of the City Council may have Kaiser coverage either
through their positions with the City, or with their own
employers.

A Kaiser Permanente medical clinic facility is
currently located in Antioch. Kaiser is considering either
acquiring, or constructing, a hospital facility within the
City. If a new facility is constructed, a number of land use
approvals would be necessary from the City, including land use
entitlements which would have to be approved by the City
Council.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
Page Two
January 2, 1990


     The nearest existing Kaiser hospitals to Antioch are
located in Martinez and Walnut Creek, both about 20 miles
distance from Antioch.

     Kaiser Permanente members are entitled to emergency
treatment at the existing private Delta Memorial Hospital in
Antioch should their injury or illness preclude them from
travelling to the Kaiser hospitals.

     Mayor Keller inquires whether he can act on the land
use approvals for the Kaiser facility irrespective of the fact
that he is a Kaiser member.  He also inquires what would
happen if the FPPC decides that there is a conflict, and the
two other Council members are similarly affected.

     Several City staff members who are also Kaiser
members would also have dealings with the project, including
members of the planning staff, building inspection,
administrative services, etc.  The Mayor also wonders whether
these employees would have a conflict.

     I have opined to the Mayor that there is no economic
benefit or detriment which would flow to himself, the other
members of the Council, or to the staff, by virtue of the
location of the hospital in Antioch.  There is the convenience
factor and slight savings of driving costs that would be
realized if a person did not have to travel to either Martinez
or Walnut Creek for hospital services.  However, these
benefits appear to be de minimis.  I have also opined that any
benefit which would be received by the Mayor would be
identical to the benefit received by the public in general if
a Kaiser hospital is constructed in the City.

     I have felt somewhat uncomfortable with my opinion,
since I am a member of Kaiser myself.  Due to the potentially
widespread effect (i.e. a majority of the Council, a large
number of City employees) the Mayor desires the advice of your
office on this matter.  Thank you.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]

                                        WILLIAM R. GALSTAN
                                        City Attorney

WRG:fl
cc:  Mayor Joel Keller
     Members of the City Council

sections.question: Mayor Keller inquires whether he can act on the land use approvals for the Kaiser facility irrespective of the fact that he is a Kaiser member. He also inquires what would happen if the FPPC decides that there is a conflict, and the two other Council members are similarly affected. The Mayor also wonders whether city staff members who are Kaiser members would have a conflict.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Mayor Keller and two other City Council members participate in land use approval decisions for a Kaiser Permanente hospital facility when they are Kaiser members themselves, and would city staff with Kaiser membership face conflicts?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/short answer section is present. The letter sets out the factual background and specific questions posed by the Mayor. The City Attorney also notes his own discomfort with his preliminary opinion due to being a Kaiser member himself.

He also inquires what would happen if the FPPC decides that there is a conflict, and the two other Council members are similarly affected. The Mayor also wonders whether city staff members who are Kaiser members would have a conflict.

embedding.summary: City Attorney William Gatstan requests FPPC advice on behalf of Antioch Mayor Joel Keller regarding whether the Mayor and other council members can participate in Kaiser Permanente facility approval decisions when they are Kaiser members and receive health insurance through the city.
